devanagari: श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराण । षष्ठः स्कन्धःदशमोऽध्यायः । देवानां दधीचेः सकाशात् तदस्थियाचनं । वज्रनिर्माणं देवदानवयुद्धं चश्रीशुक उवाचइन्द्रमे...
english_devnagari: śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇaṣaṣṭhaḥ skandhaḥdaśamo'dhyāyaḥdevānāṃ dadhīceḥ sakāśāt tadasthiyācanaṃvajranirmāṇaṃ devadānavayuddhaṃ caśrīśuka uvācaindramevaṃ samādiśya bhagavān viśvabhāvanaḥpaśyatāṃ animeṣāṇāṃ atraivāntardadhe hariḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The glorious Hari, the protector of the universe, thus instructed Indra and disappeared at that very spot, while the gods, with unwinking eyes, were simply gazing.

devanagari: तथाभियाचितो देवैः ऋषिः आथर्वणो महान् । मोदमान उवाचेदं प्रहसन्निव भारत ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tathābhiyācito devaiḥ ṛṣiḥ ātharvaṇo mahānmodamāna uvācedaṃ prahasanniva bhārata
translation: Oh Bhārata (Parīkṣit)! Solicited by gods thus (as per instructions of the Lord), the great-hearted sage of Atharvan Clan was delighted and addressed them jokingly (with a smile), as follows;

devanagari: अपि वृन्दारका यूयं न जानीथ शरीरिणाम् । संस्थायां यस्त्वभिद्रोहो दुःसहश्चेतनापहः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: api vṛndārakā yūyaṃ na jānītha śarīriṇāmsaṃsthāyāṃ yastvabhidroho duḥsahaścetanāpahaḥ
translation: “Oh gods! (Presiding deities of the senses as you are) can you not imagine the excruciating and unbearable pain caused to embodied beings at the time of death—a pain that deprives them of consciousness.

devanagari: जिजीविषूणां जीवानां आत्मा प्रेष्ठ इहेप्सितः । क उत्सहेत तं दातुं भिक्षमाणाय विष्णवे ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: jijīviṣūṇāṃ jīvānāṃ ātmā preṣṭha ihepsitaḥka utsaheta taṃ dātuṃ bhikṣamāṇāya viṣṇave
translation: In this world, (one’s own) body is the dearest thing coveted by living beings who have a desire to live. Who can dare to donate it even to Viṣṇu, should he beg for it?”

devanagari: श्रीदेवा ऊचुःकिं नु तद् दुस्त्यजं ब्रह्मन् पुंसां भूतानुकम्पिनाम् । भवद्विधानां महतां पुण्यश्लोकेड्यकर्मणाम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīdevā ūcuḥkiṃ nu tad dustyajaṃ brahman puṃsāṃ bhūtānukampināmbhavadvidhānāṃ mahatāṃ puṇyaślokeḍyakarmaṇām
translation:
Gods said:
Oh Brahman! Is there anything which is difficult to part with in the case of great persons like your honour, who are compassionate to all beings, and whose noble deeds are worthy of being praised by people with hallowing reputation.

devanagari: नूनं स्वार्थपरो लोको न वेद परसङ्कटम् । यदि वेद न याचेत नेति नाह यदीश्वरः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ svārthaparo loko na veda parasaṅkaṭamyadi veda na yāceta neti nāha yadīśvaraḥ
translation: There is no doubt that persons who are solely devoted to achieve their own objects, do not (care to) understand the danger faced by others (the donors). If they could realize it, they would not beg for it. (In the same way, the donor also does not appreciate the difficulty of the seeker of his help). If the donor were (aware of the seeker’s danger and) capable of granting it, he would never say “No”. (Hence understand our danger and grant our request).

devanagari: श्रीऋषिरुवाचधर्मं वः श्रोतुकामेन यूयं मे प्रत्युदाहृताः । एष वः प्रियमात्मानं त्यजन्तं सन्त्यजाम्यहम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīṛṣiruvācadharmaṃ vaḥ śrotukāmena yūyaṃ me pratyudāhṛtāḥeṣa vaḥ priyamātmānaṃ tyajantaṃ santyajāmyaham
translation:
The sage said:
It is with a desire to hear from you the (exposition of) Dharma (righteousness) that you were replied that way (by me). Here I cast off my body which is dear to you (due to its utility) as (some day) it is sure to leave me.

devanagari: योऽध्रुवेणात्मना नाथा न धर्मं न यशः पुमान् । ईहेत भूतदयया स शोच्यः स्थावरैरपि ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: yo'dhruveṇātmanā nāthā na dharmaṃ na yaśaḥ pumānīheta bhūtadayayā sa śocyaḥ sthāvarairapi
translation: Oh Lords! A person who does not desire to earn religious merits out of compassion to living beings or aspire after glory by means of this ephemeral body, deserves to be pitied even by immobile creatures.

devanagari: एतावानव्ययो धर्मः पुण्यश्लोकैरुपासितः । यो भूतशोकहर्षाभ्यां आत्मा शोचति हृष्यति ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvānavyayo dharmaḥ puṇyaślokairupāsitaḥyo bhūtaśokaharṣābhyāṃ ātmā śocati hṛṣyati
translation: That one should sympathetically feel sorrow or delight at the grief or joy of other beings, is the eternal law of righteousness (Dharma) practised by persons with holy renown.

devanagari: अहो दैन्यमहो कष्टं पारक्यैः क्षणभङ्गुरैः । यन्नोपकुर्यादस्वार्थैः मर्त्यः स्वज्ञातिविग्रहैः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: aho dainyamaho kaṣṭaṃ pārakyaiḥ kṣaṇabhaṅguraiḥyannopakuryādasvārthaiḥ martyaḥ svajñātivigrahaiḥ
translation: Alas! What a pity! that a mortal does not show benevolence (by helping others) with his wealth, sons and relatives and his body—all of which are transient, of no use to him and (actually) belong to others.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं कृतव्यवसितो दध्यङ्ङाथर्वणस्तनुम् । परे भगवति ब्रह्मणि आत्मानं सन्नयन्जहौ ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaevaṃ kṛtavyavasito dadhyaṅṅātharvaṇastanumpare bhagavati brahmaṇi ātmānaṃ sannayanjahau
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, Dadhyaṅ, the scion of the Atharvan Clan, made up his mind and uniting his individual Soul with the glorious transcendental Brahman, gave up his ghost.

devanagari: यताक्षासुमनोबुद्धिः तत्त्वदृग् ध्वस्तबन्धनः । आस्थितः परमं योगं न देहं बुबुधे गतम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yatākṣāsumanobuddhiḥ tattvadṛg dhvastabandhanaḥāsthitaḥ paramaṃ yogaṃ na dehaṃ bubudhe gatam
translation: He controlled his senses, vital airs, mind and intellect and fixed his gaze on the Reality. He severed all bonds and established himself in union with the Brahman, and (in the process) was not aware of the falling away of his body.

devanagari: अथेन्द्रो वज्रमुद्यम्य निर्मितं विश्वकर्मणा । मुनेः शक्तिभिरुत्सिक्तो भगवत् तेजसान्वितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: athendro vajramudyamya nirmitaṃ viśvakarmaṇāmuneḥ śaktibhirutsikto bhagavat tejasānvitaḥ
translation: Then Indra was equipped with Vajra (the thunderbolt) forged out of the bones of the sage, by Viśvakarman (the chief artisan of gods). He (Indra) was endowed with the Lord’s power and felt himself mighty.

devanagari: वृतो देवगणैः सर्वैः गजेन्द्रोपर्यशोभत । स्तूयमानो मुनिगणैः त्रैलोक्यं हर्षयन्निव ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛto devagaṇaiḥ sarvaiḥ gajendroparyaśobhatastūyamāno munigaṇaiḥ trailokyaṃ harṣayanniva
translation: Surrounded by all the hosts of gods and eulogised by assemblies of sages, he shone riding his excellent elephant (Airāvata), to the delight of the three worlds.

devanagari: वृत्रमभ्यद्रवच्छत्रुं असुरानीकयूथपैः । पर्यस्तमोजसा राजन् क्रुद्धो रुद्र इवान्तकम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛtramabhyadravacchatruṃ asurānīkayūthapaiḥparyastamojasā rājan kruddho rudra ivāntakam
translation: Inorder to cut him down, Oh King! he attacked vehemently Vṛtra who was surrounded by the leaders of Asura forces, just as wrathful Rudra does in the case of the god of Death (at the end of the world).

devanagari: ततः सुराणामसुरै रणः परमदारुणः । त्रेतामुखे नर्मदायां अभवत् प्रथमे युगे ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ surāṇāmasurai raṇaḥ paramadāruṇaḥtretāmukhe narmadāyāṃ abhavat prathame yuge
translation: Then ensued the most ferocious fight between the gods and the demons, on the bank of the Narmadā, at the beginning of the Tretā Age (in the first round of four yugas in this manvantara).

devanagari: रुद्रैर्वसुभिरादित्यैः अश्विभ्यां पितृवह्निभिः । मरुद्भिः ऋभुभिः साध्यैः विश्वेदेवैः मरुत्पतिम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: rudrairvasubhirādityaiḥ aśvibhyāṃ pitṛvahnibhiḥmarudbhiḥ ṛbhubhiḥ sādhyaiḥ viśvedevaiḥ marutpatim
translation: The demons, headed by Vṛtra, did not tolerate the sight of Indra armed with Vajra and resplendent in his original glory and surrounded by Rudras, Vasus, Ādityas, Aśvinikumāras [Aśvinīkumāras?], manes, Fire-gods, Wind-gods, Ṛbhus, Sādhyas, and Viśvedevas. on the battle-field.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा वज्रधरं शक्रं रोचमानं स्वया श्रिया । नामृष्यन्नसुरा राजन् मृधे वृत्रपुरःसराः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā vajradharaṃ śakraṃ rocamānaṃ svayā śriyānāmṛṣyannasurā rājan mṛdhe vṛtrapuraḥsarāḥ
translation: The demons, headed by Vṛtra, did not tolerate the sight of Indra armed with Vajra and resplendent in his original glory and surrounded by Rudras, Vasus, Ādityas, Aśvinikumāras [Aśvinīkumāras?], manes, Fire-gods, Wind-gods, Ṛbhus, Sādhyas, and Viśvedevas. on the battle-field.

devanagari: नमुचिः शम्बरोऽनर्वा द्विमूर्धा ऋषभोऽम्बरः । हयग्रीवः शङ्कुशिरा विप्रचित्तिः अयोमुखः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: namuciḥ śambaro'narvā dvimūrdhā ṛṣabho'mbaraḥhayagrīvaḥ śaṅkuśirā vipracittiḥ ayomukhaḥ
translation: Namuci, Śambara, Anarvā, Dvimūrdhā (a two- headed demon), Ṛṣabha, Ambara Hayagrīva (a demon with horse’s head), Śaṅkuśiras, Vipracitti, Ayomukha, Puloman, Vṛṣaparvan, Praheti, Heti, Utkala, and the sons of Diti and Danu and Yakṣas, Ogres in their thousands, of whom Sumāli and Māli were prominent—all armoured and decorated with gold ornaments—resisted the vanguard of Indra’s army which was unapproachable even to the god of Death.

devanagari: पुलोमा वृषपर्वा च प्रहेतिर्हेतिरुत्कलः । दैतेया दानवा यक्षा रक्षांसि च सहस्रशः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: pulomā vṛṣaparvā ca prahetirhetirutkalaḥdaiteyā dānavā yakṣā rakṣāṃsi ca sahasraśaḥ
translation: Namuci, Śambara, Anarvā, Dvimūrdhā (a two- headed demon), Ṛṣabha, Ambara Hayagrīva (a demon with horse’s head), Śaṅkuśiras, Vipracitti, Ayomukha, Puloman, Vṛṣaparvan, Praheti, Heti, Utkala, and the sons of Diti and Danu and Yakṣas, Ogres in their thousands, of whom Sumāli and Māli were prominent—all armoured and decorated with gold ornaments—resisted the vanguard of Indra’s army which was unapproachable even to the god of Death.

devanagari: सुमालिमालिप्रमुखाः कार्तस्वरपरिच्छदाः । प्रतिषिध्येन्द्रसेनाग्रं मृत्योरपि दुरासदम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sumālimālipramukhāḥ kārtasvaraparicchadāḥpratiṣidhyendrasenāgraṃ mṛtyorapi durāsadam
translation: Namuci, Śambara, Anarvā, Dvimūrdhā (a two- headed demon), Ṛṣabha, Ambara Hayagrīva (a demon with horse’s head), Śaṅkuśiras, Vipracitti, Ayomukha, Puloman, Vṛṣaparvan, Praheti, Heti, Utkala, and the sons of Diti and Danu and Yakṣas, Ogres in their thousands, of whom Sumāli and Māli were prominent—all armoured and decorated with gold ornaments—resisted the vanguard of Indra’s army which was unapproachable even to the god of Death.

devanagari: अभ्यर्दयन् असम्भ्रान्ताः सिंहनादेन दुर्मदाः । गदाभिः परिघैर्बाणैः प्रासमुद्‍गरतोमरैः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: abhyardayan asambhrāntāḥ siṃhanādena durmadāḥgadābhiḥ parighairbāṇaiḥ prāsamud‍garatomaraiḥ
translation: The haughty and furious demons were fearless and unbewildered. With a lion-like roar, they attacked gods with maces, iron bars, arrows, barbed shafts and javelins. They covered the leaders of gods with volleys of weapons and missiles like pikes, axes, swords, Śataghnīs (cylindrical pieces of wood, 4 cubits long and studded with iron spikes) and Bhuśuṇḍis (a Śataghnī-like weapon with iron spikes, one exceeding the other in length), on all sides.

devanagari: शूलैः परश्वधैः खड्गैः शतघ्नीभिर्भुशुण्डिभिः । सर्वतोऽवाकिरन्शस्त्रैः अस्त्रैश्च विबुधर्षभान् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śūlaiḥ paraśvadhaiḥ khaḍgaiḥ śataghnībhirbhuśuṇḍibhiḥsarvato'vākiranśastraiḥ astraiśca vibudharṣabhān
translation: The haughty and furious demons were fearless and unbewildered. With a lion-like roar, they attacked gods with maces, iron bars, arrows, barbed shafts and javelins. They covered the leaders of gods with volleys of weapons and missiles like pikes, axes, swords, Śataghnīs (cylindrical pieces of wood, 4 cubits long and studded with iron spikes) and Bhuśuṇḍis (a Śataghnī-like weapon with iron spikes, one exceeding the other in length), on all sides.

devanagari: न तेऽदृश्यन्त सञ्छन्नाः शरजालैः समन्ततः । पुङ्खानुपुङ्खपतितैः ज्योतींषीव नभोघनैः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: na te'dṛśyanta sañchannāḥ śarajālaiḥ samantataḥpuṅkhānupuṅkhapatitaiḥ jyotīṃṣīva nabhoghanaiḥ
translation: The gods, covered with net-like volleys of arrows showered on all sides with one arrow closely following the other, were rendered invisible like luminaries behind the screen of clouds in the sky.

devanagari: न ते शस्त्रास्त्रवर्षौघा ह्यासेदुः सुरसैनिकान् । छिन्नाः सिद्धपथे देवैः लघुहस्तैः सहस्रधा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: na te śastrāstravarṣaughā hyāseduḥ surasainikānchinnāḥ siddhapathe devaiḥ laghuhastaiḥ sahasradhā
translation: The streams of the volleys of missiles and weapons did not reach or touch the soldiers in the army of gods, for they were cut down to pieces in thousands on their way in the sky, by gods with quick hands.

devanagari: अथ क्षीणास्त्रशस्त्रौघा गिरिश्रृङ्गद्रुमोपलैः । अभ्यवर्षन् सुरबलं चिच्छिदुस्तांश्च पूर्ववत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: atha kṣīṇāstraśastraughā giriśrṛṅgadrumopalaiḥabhyavarṣan surabalaṃ cicchidustāṃśca pūrvavat
translation: When their stock of missiles and weapons was exhausted, they showered the army of gods with mountain peaks, trees and stones, and the gods cut them to pieces as before.

devanagari: तानक्षतान् स्वस्तिमतो निशाम्य । शस्त्रास्त्रपूगैरथ वृत्रनाथाः । द्रुमैर्दृषद्‍भिर्विविधाद्रिश्रृङ्गैः । अविक्षतान् तत्रसुरिन्द्रसैनिकान...
english_devnagari: tānakṣatān svastimato niśāmyaśastrāstrapūgairatha vṛtranāthāḥdrumairdṛṣad‍bhirvividhādriśrṛṅgaiḥavikṣatān tatrasurindrasainikān
translation: Finding that the gods were unhurt and safe despite the volleys of weapons and missiles, and unaffected by trees, shower of stones and different mountain peaks, the demons led by Vṛtra were filled with consternation.

devanagari: सर्वे प्रयासा अभवन्विमोघाः । कृताः कृता देवगणेषु दैत्यैः । कृष्णानुकूलेषु यथा महत्सु । क्षुद्रैः प्रयुक्ता ऊषती रूक्षवाचः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve prayāsā abhavanvimoghāḥkṛtāḥ kṛtā devagaṇeṣu daityaiḥkṛṣṇānukūleṣu yathā mahatsukṣudraiḥ prayuktā ūṣatī rūkṣavācaḥ
translation: Just as abusive language employed by the vulgar against the exalted persons does not disturb them, all the efforts repeatedly made against the army of gods by the demons became ineffectual, as Lord Viṣṇu was favourable to them.

devanagari: ते स्वप्रयासं वितथं निरीक्ष्य । हरावभक्ता हतयुद्धदर्पाः । पलायनायाजिमुखे विसृज्य । पतिं मनस्ते दधुरात्तसाराः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: te svaprayāsaṃ vitathaṃ nirīkṣyaharāvabhaktā hatayuddhadarpāḥpalāyanāyājimukhe visṛjyapatiṃ manaste dadhurāttasārāḥ
translation: The demons who were not devoted to Hari, had their arrogance and pride of fighting crushed, when they found that all their efforts had become infructuous. With their morale deeply affected, they thought of fleeing from the battlefield deserting their leader at the initial stage of fighting.

devanagari: वृत्रोऽसुरान् तान् अनुगान् मनस्वी । प्रधावतः प्रेक्ष्य बभाष एतत् । पलायितं प्रेक्ष्य बलं च भग्नं । भयेन तीव्रेण विहस्य वीरः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vṛtro'surān tān anugān manasvīpradhāvataḥ prekṣya babhāṣa etatpalāyitaṃ prekṣya balaṃ ca bhagnaṃbhayena tīvreṇa vihasya vīraḥ
translation: The self-possessed warrior Vṛtra saw that his followers are running away. Finding his army broken through terror and fleeing, he laughed loudly and spoke.

devanagari: कालोपपन्नां रुचिरां मनस्विनां । मुवाच वाचं पुरुषप्रवीरः । हे विप्रचित्ते नमुचे पुलोमन् । मयानर्वन्छम्बर मे श्रृणुध्वम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kālopapannāṃ rucirāṃ manasvināṃmuvāca vācaṃ puruṣapravīraḥhe vipracitte namuce pulomanmayānarvanchambara me śrṛṇudhvam
translation:
The heroic person spoke the following words appropriate to the occasion and agreeable to the high-minded souls:
“Oh Vipracitti, Namuci, Puloman, Maya, Anarvan, Śambara, listen to me.

devanagari: जातस्य मृत्युर्ध्रुव एव सर्वतः । प्रतिक्रिया यस्य न चेह कॢप्ता । लोको यशश्चाथ ततो यदि ह्यमुं । को नाम मृत्युं न वृणीत युक्तम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: jātasya mṛtyurdhruva eva sarvataḥpratikriyā yasya na ceha kḷptāloko yaśaścātha tato yadi hyamuṃko nāma mṛtyuṃ na vṛṇīta yuktam
translation: This death is inevitable to whoever is born. No escape from any side from this, has been devised (by āny one). If the attainment of heaven and glory could follow death, who would not court such a desirable and welcome end?

devanagari: द्वौ सम्मताविह मृत्यू दुरापौ । यद्‍ब्रह्मसन्धारणया जितासुः । कलेवरं योगरतो विजह्याद् । यदग्रणीर्वीरशयेऽनिवृत्तः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: dvau sammatāviha mṛtyū durāpauyad‍brahmasandhāraṇayā jitāsuḥkalevaraṃ yogarato vijahyādyadagraṇīrvīraśaye'nivṛttaḥ
translation: Two modes of death are approved of (by the great), but they do not easily come to one’s lot, namely, (1) he who, engaged in the Yogic process, controls his vital airs, concentrates himself on Brahman and casts off his body and (2) one who, in the fore-front of battle, gives up his body on the battlefield (the bed of warriors), without turning his back.”
